description: Introduce explicit reference-derived write metadata in the relational write-plan contracts for both propagated reference identity column bindings and key-unification member inventory. Acceptance: propagated reference identity bindings and unified reference-derived members are no longer represented as ordinary JSON scalar reads; the external contract, DTO, codec, and manifest-summary layers can identify each derived binding or member by reference site plus logical reference-member path; later key-unification work can consume the new contract shape without reopening serialization changes; the design continues to use existing `ResolvedReferenceSet` and `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` rather than widening the resolver payload; and compiler, codec, or runtime mismatches in the new metadata shape fail fast as internal invariant breaches rather than degrading to generic scalar metadata.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `NormalizedPlanContractCodec` and `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter` coverage so reference-derived column bindings and key-unification members survive round-tripping, appear in normalized and manifest output, and invalid DTO or manifest mutations fail fast instead of silently collapsing back to generic scalar shape.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `WritePlanCompiler` and related helpers to compile non-unified propagated reference identity bindings from explicit reference-derived sources, plus any shared source-kind plumbing that flattening and later unified-member planning require. Acceptance: non-unified propagated scalar identity columns compile from reference-derived sources instead of `WriteValueSource.Scalar`; descriptor-backed propagated identity bindings compile from descriptor-document-id storage rather than raw URI strings; the compiler emits the shared provenance or source metadata needed downstream without taking ownership of key-unified member precedence; and any narrow model or DDL cleanup stays limited to what this compilation work requires.
steps-to-verify: Extend `Given_WritePlanCompiler_BindingsAndSources` coverage for direct propagated reference identity columns and descriptor-backed reference identity members.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_BindingsAndSources"`

description: Extend `KeyUnificationWritePlanCompiler` so key-unified reference identity sites use reference-derived producers instead of the old JSON-bound scalar path. Acceptance: canonical storage columns for unified reference identity parts are computed from explicit reference-derived members; mixed classes can include both ordinary JSON-bound members and reference-derived members without contract churn; reference-derived members take deterministic precedence when both can feed the same canonical storage column; descriptor-backed unified members resolve to descriptor `DocumentId` values; optional or absent reference sites keep presence-gated aliases semantically absent; and reference-derived provenance remains discoverable by reference site plus member path for follow-on profile accounting in DMS-1124.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `Given_WritePlanCompiler_KeyUnification` tests for reference-derived canonical producers, one mixed-source case proving reference-derived precedence over an ordinary JSON-bound member for the same canonical column, descriptor-backed members, and one optional absent-site case.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_KeyUnification"`

description: Extend the flattening-time resolved-reference lookup adapter to expose propagated identity-part values without changing the external resolver contract. Acceptance: the compiled reference-derived binding order is the authoritative bridge between local propagated bindings and ordered `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` values for the matched resolved reference occurrence; no path-matching implementation is introduced between reference-object member paths and target-identity paths; scalar identity parts are sourced from ordered `DocumentReference.DocumentIdentity` values; descriptor-backed identity parts are sourced from existing descriptor-resolution results and materialize local descriptor `DocumentId` values; nested collection occurrences resolve by ordinal path; and count or order drift between compiled metadata and resolved reference payloads fails deterministically instead of silently falling back to request JSON.
steps-to-verify: Add focused backend unit coverage for root and nested reference lookups, descriptor-backed propagated members, and count or order invariant-breach cases.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `RelationalWriteFlattener` to materialize propagated reference identity columns and unified canonical storage columns from resolved reference-derived values on both create and existing-document flows. Acceptance: root and nested reference sites populate `..._DocumentId` plus propagated identity storage from resolved reference data rather than raw body scalars; descriptor-backed and key-unified reference identity members materialize the correct canonical or storage columns and presence flags; one explicit optional-absent-path case proves aliases remain semantically absent while canonical storage stays the real propagation target; and impossible reference-derived mismatches fail fast instead of degrading to generic scalar reads.
steps-to-verify: Extend `Given_RelationalWriteFlattener` tests for direct propagated bindings, unified reference-derived members, descriptor-backed reference members, and optional absent-site behavior.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_RelationalWriteFlattener"`

description: Add focused PostgreSQL integration proof for the core DMS-985 runtime population path. Acceptance: one POST create scenario asserts persisted propagated reference identity columns for at least one non-unified reference site and one descriptor-backed reference identity member; one existing-document scenario changes the referenced target or referenced identity part so `..._DocumentId` and propagated identity storage are re-populated from resolved references rather than merely preserved; the focused fixture stays centered on runtime-populated persisted columns rather than cascade or stamping side effects; and the proof remains small enough to isolate plain write-path regressions from downstream propagation behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add a focused PostgreSQL integration fixture or test class dedicated to propagated reference identity columns so create and changed-`PUT` persisted-column behavior are asserted directly.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Propagated_Reference_Identity_Runtime"`

description: Add focused PostgreSQL proof that runtime-populated propagated reference identity columns satisfy the required propagation shape. Acceptance: rows created through the backend write path satisfy the per-reference all-or-none storage shape without taking ownership of new DB-exception classification; at least one scenario seeds a referrer through the runtime write path and then mutates the referenced identity so PostgreSQL cascade updates the stored propagated columns populated by that write; and this proof stays separate from the plain persisted-column fixture so cascade or constraint failures are isolated cleanly.
steps-to-verify: Add a focused PostgreSQL integration fixture or test class that seeds a referrer through the write path, then verifies all-or-none storage and a subsequent referenced-identity cascade on the populated propagated columns.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Propagated_Reference_Identity_Cascade"`

description: Keep the authoritative PostgreSQL `StudentSchoolAssociation` runtime smoke as the story's end-to-end sample, but scope this task to targeted verification or gap-filling rather than a broad new fixture. Acceptance: review the existing create and changed-`PUT` assertions and extend them only where the new reference-derived runtime source of truth is not already being asserted on the root association row or the alternative-graduation-plan child rows; repeat-`PUT` no-op behavior remains unchanged; and the authoritative sample stays a small follow-up proof beside the focused PostgreSQL runtime fixture rather than duplicating it.
steps-to-verify: Tighten the existing authoritative PostgreSQL sample assertions only where the new reference-derived behavior is not already covered on create and changed `PUT`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~StudentSchoolAssociation"`

category: SQL Server DDL And Trigger Verification
description: Keep SQL Server proof on the DDL or trigger surface and make the story's SQL Server work a concrete authoritative-smoke task. Acceptance: update `MssqlGeneratedDdlAuthoritativeSmokeTests` so the existing trigger-fallback propagation scenario explicitly proves the canonical or storage columns relevant to DMS-985 are updated through SQL Server's trigger-maintained propagation shape; keep the work confined to authoritative DDL or trigger assertions in that surface area; and do not introduce a separate SQL Server runtime repository scenario unless the implementation ends up touching dialect-specific executor behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add or adjust a narrow assertion in `MssqlGeneratedDdlAuthoritativeSmokeTests` so the existing trigger-fallback propagation proof explicitly covers the relevant canonical or storage columns for this story.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Identity_propagation"`

description: Close the gap between Core reference extraction and backend flattening so propagated-reference write requests enforce the story's all-or-none semantics with deterministic request-validation failures instead of internal invariant exceptions. Acceptance: an empty reference object and any partially populated propagated reference object are both rejected as `Data Validation Failed` with concrete JSON-path keyed validation errors; Core no longer treats a present-but-empty reference object as if the optional reference site were absent; backend flattening no longer throws `InvalidOperationException` when a reference object is present but the resolved lookup set has no matching occurrence; missing reference-derived values and missing document-reference lookups surface as `WriteValidationFailure` entries rather than uncaught internal errors; and at least one POST plus one PUT scenario prove the final response shape is stable for root and nested reference sites.
steps-to-verify: Add Core coverage around `ReferenceExtractor` or the write-validation middleware path for an empty reference object and a partially populated reference object, asserting concrete path-specific validation failures instead of silent omission or raw `InvalidOperationException` behavior.

Add backend unit coverage in `RelationalWriteFlattenerTests` or executor-level tests for present reference objects whose lookup entries are missing, asserting `RelationalWriteRequestValidationException` and path-specific `WriteValidationFailure` output for direct document-reference bindings and reference-derived members.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Reference"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteFlattener|FullyQualifiedName~DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor"`

description: Add SQL Server runtime write-path coverage that proves propagated reference identity columns are populated correctly by relational POST and PUT execution, not just by DDL-trigger propagation over pre-seeded rows. Acceptance: at least one SQL Server integration fixture exercises the real relational repository or write-executor path for create and changed-update flows on a resource with propagated reference identity columns; the test asserts persisted `..._DocumentId` and propagated identity-part storage columns immediately after POST and after a changed PUT; the proof is parallel in intent to the existing PostgreSQL `StudentSchoolAssociation` runtime test rather than another DDL-only harness; and at least one assertion distinguishes true runtime re-population from simply preserving previously seeded values.
steps-to-verify: Add a focused SQL Server integration test or fixture that performs a relational create and changed update through the runtime write path, then reads the persisted row state and asserts the propagated reference identity columns.

If the fixture also covers identity propagation fallback after runtime creation, update the referenced identity after the write-path seed and assert the runtime-populated referrer row receives the expected propagated update through SQL Server's trigger fallback.

description: Reduce maintenance surface introduced by the propagated-reference implementation by consolidating duplicated `ReferenceDerived` support code in test-only serialization helpers and the runtime flattener's scalar-conversion path. Acceptance: `ReferenceDerived` JSON or manifest encoding logic is expressed through shared helper routines rather than repeated bespoke branches in multiple plan-test utilities; the flattener uses one authoritative scalar-conversion pathway for both ordinary JSON scalar reads and resolved reference-derived scalar values, keeping type parsing and error-shaping consistent; no existing `ReferenceDerived` contract or runtime behavior regresses; and the cleanup does not broaden the story beyond serialization helpers and flattener conversion reuse.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the plan-test utility layers so `NormalizedPlanContractCodec`, `NormalizedPlanDtoJson`, and `MappingSetManifestJsonEmitter` share the `ReferenceDerived` serialization or decoding logic instead of carrying parallel bespoke implementations.

Refactor `RelationalWriteFlattener` so reference-derived scalar conversion reuses the same parsing rules and invalid-value error semantics as the main scalar conversion pipeline, then extend unit coverage to lock the behavior.

description: Harden propagated-reference identity extraction so malformed present members fail as deterministic write-validation errors instead of escaping as internal `500` exceptions. Acceptance: when a propagated reference identity member is present but `null`, a JSON object, or a JSON array, Core returns `400 Data Validation Failed` with a concrete JSON-path keyed validation error instead of allowing `InvalidOperationException` from `SelectNodesAndLocationFromArrayPathCoerceToStrings()` or `ReferenceExtractor` to escape; the behavior is consistent for root and nested reference sites; malformed present members are reported as invalid-present values rather than silently treated as missing optional members; empty reference objects and partial reference objects continue to report all-or-none failures deterministically; and middleware only maps request-shape validation failures, leaving true invariant breaches as internal errors.
steps-to-verify: Update `ReferenceExtractor` and any supporting JSON helpers so reference identity extraction can distinguish missing members from present-but-invalid members and accumulate `WriteValidationFailure` entries for malformed values at the concrete member path.

Add focused unit coverage in `ReferenceExtractorTests` for root and nested propagated reference identity members with `null`, `{}`, and `[]` values, asserting `ReferenceExtractionValidationException` with deterministic path-specific failures.

Add middleware coverage in `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddlewareTests` proving POST and PUT requests with malformed propagated reference identity members return `400` validation responses instead of uncaught `InvalidOperationException` behavior.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceExtractor|FullyQualifiedName~ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware"`

description: Preserve supported same-site duplicate `DocumentReferenceBinding.IdentityBindings[*].ReferenceJsonPath` members throughout propagated-reference extraction and runtime materialization. Acceptance: duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` values within one reference site and one table remain supported as the design requires; `ReferenceExtractor` no longer overwrites earlier `DocumentIdentityElement` values when duplicate reference-member paths are present and instead preserves compiled binding order for referential-id calculation and downstream resolution; runtime lookup and flattening no longer resolve a duplicate logical member by arbitrarily picking the first matching binding index; propagated scalar and descriptor-backed identity columns sourced from the same logical reference path can both materialize correctly; and duplicate-path handling remains isolated to same-site groups without merging across different reference sites or tables.
steps-to-verify: Update `ReferenceExtractor` so per-reference identity collection preserves ordered entries for duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` bindings instead of keying them only by path string.

Update runtime reference-derived lookup metadata and `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet` so duplicate logical member paths are resolved by authoritative compiled binding or column identity rather than `ReferenceJsonPath -> first index` lookup.
Add Core unit coverage proving `ExtractReferences` preserves ordered `DocumentIdentityElements` and referential-id inputs when one reference site contains duplicate `referenceJsonPaths` entries.

Add backend unit coverage in `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSetTests` and `RelationalWriteFlattenerTests` proving two propagated identity columns that share one logical reference path both materialize correctly for scalar and descriptor-backed cases.

description: Normalize descriptor-valued identity members inside document references before referential-id calculation so reference-bearing identities follow the same case-insensitive descriptor behavior as standalone descriptor references. Acceptance: when a reference identity includes a descriptor URI member such as `$.graduationPlanReference.graduationPlanTypeDescriptor`, the extracted `DocumentIdentityElement` value is normalized consistently with descriptor extraction before `ReferentialId` hashing; POST and PUT reference resolution succeed for equivalent descriptor URIs regardless of input casing; the fix is limited to descriptor-valued identity members and does not alter scalar identity hashing semantics; and unit or integration coverage proves mixed-case payloads resolve to the same target row as lowercase payloads.
steps-to-verify: Update Core reference-identity extraction so descriptor-valued members under document references are normalized before building `DocumentIdentity` and `ReferentialId`.
Add focused Core or backend coverage for a resource whose identity contains a descriptor-valued reference member, proving mixed-case and lowercase payloads resolve identically.

description: Constrain propagated-reference request-shape validation to the intended relational write surface so DMS-985 does not silently change legacy write behavior outside the relational backend seam. Acceptance: malformed or partial propagated reference objects still produce deterministic validation failures on the relational path, but the implementation no longer forces a new middleware-level failure mode onto unrelated or legacy write flows unless that behavior is explicitly intended and documented; `UseRelationalBackend=false` behavior remains unchanged or is covered by an intentional compatibility decision with tests; and the final design makes the responsibility boundary between Core extraction and relational write validation explicit instead of leaving it as branch-local scope creep.
steps-to-verify: Review the current `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` integration and move, gate, or otherwise isolate propagated-reference validation so only the intended write path owns the new behavior.
Add tests that prove relational writes still return concrete path-specific validation errors for empty or partial reference objects.
Add regression coverage for the non-relational or legacy path showing either unchanged behavior or an explicitly approved new behavior with test documentation.

description: Fix the real relational request path so descriptor-valued propagated reference identity members are resolved without test-only augmentation. Acceptance: a normal Core-fronted POST or PUT that includes descriptor-valued members under `documentPathsMapping.referenceJsonPaths` supplies all descriptor lookup inputs needed by `ReferenceResolver`, `FlatteningResolvedReferenceLookupSet`, and `RelationalWriteFlattener` without any hand-built `DescriptorReference` additions in tests; descriptor-valued reference identity members under root and collection scopes resolve to the correct descriptor `DocumentId` and populate the expected propagated identity storage columns; whichever layer owns the fix (`ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware`, Core extraction, or a relational-path adapter after write-plan selection) derives the descriptor resource type and concrete JSON path deterministically from authoritative schema or plan metadata rather than brittle path guessing; missing or incompatible descriptor values continue to surface as normal descriptor validation failures keyed to the concrete member path; and the authoritative `StudentSchoolAssociation` runtime scenario no longer relies on `CreateAlternativeGraduationPlanDescriptorReferences()` or any equivalent workaround to make the branch pass.

description: Restore the documented `UseRelationalBackend=false` behavior so DMS-985 does not change legacy document-store request semantics while still keeping relational-path validation intact. Acceptance: when `UseRelationalBackend=true`, malformed present reference objects and partial propagated-reference identities still fail deterministically as `400 Data Validation Failed` responses with concrete JSON-path keyed validation errors; when `UseRelationalBackend=false`, the runtime behavior for empty, partial, or malformed reference objects matches pre-DMS-985 behavior rather than silently dropping extracted references or changing the downstream request shape; the middleware boundary makes it explicit which extraction or validation rules belong only to the relational write path; new tests prove the flag-on relational behavior and the flag-off legacy behavior separately; and no legacy-path response or `DocumentInfo` regression remains locked in by branch-local tests that contradict the DMS-983 acceptance criteria.
steps-to-verify: Refactor `ExtractDocumentInfoMiddleware` and any supporting extraction logic so relational request-shape validation is only applied when the relational backend seam is active, while the legacy path preserves the pre-branch extraction and failure semantics.

Replace or update the new legacy-compatibility tests that currently assert silent omission of malformed references, and add parity coverage that documents the intended pre-DMS-985 legacy behavior for empty and malformed reference objects.

Add one flag-on middleware test and one flag-off middleware or handler-path test for both a root reference and a nested reference so the scope boundary is explicit and protected against future regressions.

description: Consolidate duplicated reference-derived source-path validation so write-plan compilation and key-unification compilation use one authoritative helper and one stable diagnostic contract. Acceptance: the path-consistency check currently implemented in both `WritePlanCompiler.CreateReferenceDerivedSource` and `KeyUnificationWritePlanCompiler.ValidateReferenceDerivedSourcePath` is expressed once in shared code; both compiler stages continue to reject mismatches between `DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath` and `ReferenceIdentityBinding.ReferenceJsonPath`; exception wording stays deterministic and intentionally aligned across both call sites; the shared helper remains narrow to this validation concern rather than broad compiler refactoring; and unit coverage proves both ordinary propagated bindings and key-unified reference-derived members still fail fast with the expected message when source metadata drifts.
steps-to-verify: Extract the common reference-derived source-path validation into a shared compiler helper or support type used by both `WritePlanCompiler` and `KeyUnificationWritePlanCompiler`.
